title: The Latent Class Model
text: Latent Class Analysis assumes people belong different groups (.e., classes) due nonobservable characteristics. latent class model, aim estimate two kinds probabilities: probability person belongs particular class. conditional probabilities item responses person belongs given class.

Hence, ‚Ąď=(‚ąĎk=1KP(xk)‚ąŹj=1JP(yj‚ą£xk))n, \ell \;=\; \Bigg(\sum_{k=1}^{K} P(x_k)\, \prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_k)\Bigg)^{\!n},  log-likelihood becomes ‚Ąď‚Ąď=nlog‚Ā°(‚ąĎk=1KP(xk)‚ąŹj=1JP(yj‚ą£xk)). \ell\ell \;=\; n \log \Bigg(\sum_{k=1}^{K} P(x_k)\, \prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_k)\Bigg).  term inside parenthesis probability single pattern, P(ūĚź≤)P(\mathbf{y}). text: classes g,hg,h, ‚ąā2‚Ąď‚Ąď‚ąāP(xg)‚ąāP(xh)=‚ąín1P(ūĚź≤)2(‚ąŹj=1JP(yj‚ą£xg))(‚ąŹj=1JP(yj‚ą£xh))=‚ąínP(xg‚ą£y)P(xh‚ą£y)P(xg)P(xh). \begin{aligned} \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial P(x_g)\,\partial P(x_h)} \;&=\; -\,n \,\frac{1}{P(\mathbf{y})^{2}} \, \Bigg(\prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_g)\Bigg)\! \Bigg(\prod_{j=1}^{J} P(y_j \mid x_h)\Bigg) \\ &=\; -n \frac{P(x_g \mid y) P(x_h \mid y)}{P(x_g) P(x_h)}. \end{aligned} items m,nm,n classes g,hg,h, ‚ąā2‚Ąď‚Ąď‚ąālym‚ą£xg‚ąālyn‚ą£xh={nP(xg‚ą£y)(1‚ąíP(xg‚ą£y)),g=h,‚ąínP(xg‚ą£y)P(xh‚ą£y),otherwise. \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}\,\partial l_{y_n \mid x_h}} = \begin{cases} \,n \, P(x_g \mid y) \!\ \big(1-P(x_g \mid y)\big), & \text{} \!\ g=h,\\[1.25em] -\,n \, P(x_g \mid y) \!\ P(x_h \mid y), & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases} mixed second derivative, ‚ąā2‚Ąď‚Ąď‚ąāP(xh)‚ąālym‚ą£xg={1P(xg)nP(xg‚ą£y)(1‚ąíP(xg‚ą£y)),g=h,‚ąí1P(xh)nP(xg‚ą£y)P(xh‚ą£y),otherwise. \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial P(x_h)\,\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}} = \begin{cases} \frac{1}{P(x_g)} \, n \, P(x_g \mid y) \big(1-P(x_g \mid y)\big), & \text{} g=h,\\[1.0em] -\, \frac{1}{P(x_h)} \, n \, P(x_g \mid y) P(x_h \mid y), & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases} Collecting terms gives Hessian block form: Hess(‚Ąď‚Ąď)=[‚ąā2‚Ąď‚Ąď‚ąāP(xg)‚ąāP(xh)‚ąā2‚Ąď‚Ąď‚ąāP(xh)‚ąālym‚ą£xg‚ąā2‚Ąď‚Ąď‚ąālym‚ą£xg‚ąāP(xh)‚ąā2‚Ąď‚Ąď‚ąālym‚ą£xg‚ąālym‚ą£xg]. \mathrm{Hess}(\ell\ell) \;=\; \begin{bmatrix} \displaystyle \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial P(x_g)\,\partial P(x_h)} & \displaystyle \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial P(x_h)\,\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}} \\[0.8em] \displaystyle \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}\,\partial P(x_h)} & \displaystyle \frac{\partial^2 \ell\ell}{\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}\,\partial l_{y_m \mid x_g}} \end{bmatrix}\!.

title: Local Bivariate Residuals for Latent Class Analysis ‚ÄĒ lbvr
text: list class `"lbvr"` components: residual_matrix symmetric matrix residual measures: continuous‚Äďcontinuous pairs: Pearson correlation residuals. categorical‚Äďcategorical pairs: maximum absolute standardized residual. continuous‚Äďcategorical pairs: maximum absolute Z‚ÄĎscore mean residuals per category. pvalue_matrix Symmetric matrix p-values corresponding     residual measures (using correlation test, chi‚ÄĎsquare test, ANOVA F‚ÄĎtest). r_mat Symmetric matrix effect sizes comparable correlation: Continuous‚Äďcontinuous: Pearson \(r\). Categorical‚Äďcategorical: difference Cram√©r's V (\(V_{obs} - V_{exp}\)). Continuous‚Äďcategorical: \(\eta\) (square root eta‚ÄĎsquared ANOVA). rmsr Root mean square upper triangle `r_mat`; global fit index. res_tab data frame one row per variable pair, containing:     pair, statistic (residual measure), p_value,     r (correlation‚ÄĎlike effect size), var_types,     test (descriptive label). table sorted absolute r.

title: Latent Class Analysis ‚ÄĒ lca
text: lca() estimates latent class models measurement model may contain Gaussian indicators, multinomial indicators, . Class membership probabilities can unconditional modeled softmax transformation observed covariates. Distal outcomes can also included modeled conditionally latent classes. function supports one-step estimation, Bakk‚ÄďKuha two-step estimation, ML three-step estimation modal proportional classification, class enumeration passing several values nclasses.

title: Latent Class Analysis ‚ÄĒ lca
text: data data.frame containing observed variables. Variables listed gaussian /multinomial used indicators. Variables listed covariates used predict class membership. Variables listed outcomes treated distal outcomes. nclasses positive integer giving number latent classes. vector positive integers supplied, one model fitted value result returned object class "llcalist". usually termed "class enumeration". gaussian Optional character vector names variables data modeled Gaussian indicators. variables modeled class-specific means variances. residual covariance terms involving Gaussian indicators specified model, corresponding variables modeled jointly class-specific multivariate Gaussian distribution. multinomial Optional character vector names variables data modeled multinomial indicators. variables converted factors internally. first factor level used reference category softmax transformation. Variables 30 observed categories allowed estimated probabilities unreliable. covariates Optional character vector names variables data used predict latent class membership. Internally, intercept always included. Character covariates converted factors factors dummy-coded using model.matrix(). Observations missing values covariates removed estimation. outcomes Optional distal outcomes. can either character vector variable names data, named list specifying likelihood outcome. character vector supplied, numeric variables treated Gaussian outcomes non-numeric variables multinomial outcomes. list supplied, supported names gaussian multinomial. example, outcomes = c("y1", "y2", "z") automatically assigns numeric outcomes Gaussian likelihood non-numeric outcomes multinomial likelihood; outcomes = list(gaussian = c("y1", "y2")) adds Gaussian distal outcomes; outcomes = list(multinomial = "z") adds multinomial distal outcome. possible predict different types outcomes time using outcomes = list(gaussian = c("y1", "y2"), multinomial = "z") model Optional model specification. can named list used fix parameters, impose equality constraints, provide custom parameter labels. Names match internal parameter blocks, example beta, Gaussian item names, log_<item> multinomial logs, means, logsigma, sigma|Class<> multivariate Gaussian blocks. Character strings containing residual-dependency syntax "y1 ~~ y2" "u1 ~~ u2 ~~ u3" also used identify residual covariances residual associations. object class "llca" supplied, measurement parameters reused class-membership regression coefficients re-estimated. weights Optional numeric vector observation weights, one value per row data. adjustment Character string selecting estimation strategy covariates supplied. Use "none" one-step estimation, "bk" Bakk‚ÄďKuha two-step method, "ml" ML three-step correction based classification error. Defaults "bk". classification Character string used adjustment = "ml". Use "modal" modal assignment "prop" proportional assignment estimating classification-error matrix. penalties Logical value named list controlling regularization. FALSE, penalties used. TRUE, default penalties used. named list supplied, missing penalty blocks filled defaults. Valid penalty blocks beta, class, prob, var, Sigma. default list(beta = list(alpha = 0), class = list(alpha = 1), prob = list(alpha = 1), var = list(alpha = 1), Sigma = list(alpha = 1)). beta block may also contain lambda power ridge-type regularization. start Optional named list starting values. Names correspond parameter blocks model. Supplied values replace corresponding default initial values, allowing partial specification starting values. control Optional list optimizer estimation controls. Common entries include rstarts number random starts, cores parallel computation, maxit maximum number optimizer iterations, opt optimizer type, convergence tolerances eps, df_eps, step_eps. Missing entries replaced internal defaults. .fit Logical. TRUE, model estimated. FALSE, function returns "llca" object containing processed data, model structure, optimization setup, without running optimizer. verbose Logical. TRUE, progress information printed, especially fitting several values nclasses.

title: Latent Class Analysis ‚ÄĒ lca
text: Estimate latent class models continuous categorical indicators, optional covariates distal outcomes. measurement model defined variables supplied gaussian multinomial. Variables listed ignored measurement model unless used covariates distal outcomes. Gaussian indicators, model estimates class-specific means variances. Variances parameterized log-variances transformed positive scale optimization. multinomial indicators, model estimates class-specific category probabilities softmax parameterization. first category fixed zero log scale identification. covariates supplied adjustment = "none", class probabilities modeled one step multinomial-log coefficients stored beta parameter block. first class reference class, coefficients fixed zero. adjustment = "bk", measurement model first estimated without covariates. structural model estimated measurement parameters fixed. adjustment = "ml", measurement model first estimated, cases assigned classes using either modal proportional classification, structural model estimated using classification-error correction. function compresses data unique response/covariate patterns uses frequencies pattern weights. observation weights supplied weights, incorporated pattern weights. Residual dependencies can requested covariance-style syntax model. Gaussian indicators, terms "y1 ~~ y2" define class-specific residual covariance parameters. multinomial indicators, dependency syntax defines joint categorical response blocks. example, "u1 ~~ u2" replaces locally independent likelihood contribution u1 u2 joint multinomial contribution u1.u2. Similarly, connected dependency "u1 ~~ u2 ~~ u3" defines one joint variable u1.u2.u3. Pairwise interaction parameters used build joint log-probabilities, marginal item probabilities recovered joint probabilities summing relevant joint levels.
